compiled_state.title: Add legacy comment to ci-failure-validation-state.md
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
GREEN

## Description
Add a legacy comment adjacent to the lint_ruff and lint_mypy field definitions in ci-failure-validation-state.md explaining they are Python-specific legacy keys preserved for validate.sh compatibility.

The comment should be placed within the JSON schema block, adjacent to the field definitions. Example:

```json
"lint_ruff": "pass|fail",   // Python-specific legacy key — preserved for validate.sh (lines 125-126)
"lint_mypy": "pass|fail",   // Python-specific legacy key — preserved for validate.sh (lines 125-126)
```

Or as a markdown comment block above the schema if inline JSON comments would break schema documentation.

## TDD Exemption
Criterion 3 — static asset only. This is a Markdown schema documentation file with no conditional logic. Any test would be a change-detector (asserting the comment text exists). The structural boundary (JSON schema validity) is unchanged by adding a documentation comment.

## Files to Modify
- plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/ci-failure-validation-state.md (EDIT)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ci-failure-validation-state.md contains legacy comment text near lint_ruff/lint_mypy
  Verify: grep -q 'legacy\|Legacy\|Python-specific' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/ci-failure-validation-state.md
- [ ] lint_ruff and lint_mypy field names still present in the file
  Verify: grep -q 'lint_ruff' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/ci-failure-validation-state.md && grep -q 'lint_mypy' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/prompts/ci-failure-validation-state.md
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/run-all.sh
